enrichment.short_description: Founderpath provides non-dilutive revenue financing, term loans, and merchant cash advances to software and CPG founders.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Founderpath offers non-dilutive revenue financing, term loans, and cash advances so founders raise capital without giving up equity.

features.title: Revenue financing
features.description: Discount rates starting from 7% for companies with $1M-$3M ARR, with funding available in as little as 24 hours.

features.title: Term loans
features.description: Interest rates starting from 15% for companies with $3M+ revenue, with repayment terms of up to 4 years.

features.title: Merchant cash advance
features.description: Repayment set from 5% of monthly revenue, designed for seasonal businesses.

features.title: Data-driven underwriting
features.description: Qualification is based on business data rather than a pitch deck, with fast underwriting in 24-48 hours.

features.title: No personal guarantees or board seats
features.description: Financing is secured by a lien on business assets only, without personal guarantees, board seats, or warrants.

features.title: Early repayment without penalty
features.description: Founders can repay financing early without incurring additional penalty fees.

pros: Non-dilutive capital that preserves founder equity
Funding decisions and disbursement can happen within 24-48 hours
No personal guarantees, board seats, or warrants required
Multiple financing structures (revenue financing, term loans, cash advances) for different business stages
4.9/5 rating on Trustpilot from over 113 verified founder reviews
cons: Term loan interest rates start at 15%, which is high relative to traditional bank financing
Requires a minimum of $500K in last-year revenue to qualify
Pricing and eligibility are underwriting-based rather than published as fixed plans
Headquarters and team size are not disclosed on the website

faqs.question: What types of financing does Founderpath offer?
faqs.answer: Founderpath offers revenue financing, term loans, and merchant cash advances, each structured differently depending on company revenue and business type.

faqs.question: Does Founderpath require equity or a pitch deck?
faqs.answer: No. Founderpath uses data-driven underwriting instead of a pitch deck and provides non-dilutive capital that does not require giving up equity.

faqs.question: How fast can I get funded?
faqs.answer: Revenue financing can be funded in as little as 24 hours after underwriting is complete.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for Founderpath financing?
faqs.answer: Founderpath targets software, brick-and-mortar, and CPG businesses with at least $500K in last-year revenue, healthy retention, and recurring or repeat revenue.

faqs.question: Are there penalties for paying off financing early?
faqs.answer: No, Founderpath allows early repayment without penalty.

use_cases.title: Growth financing for SaaS companies
use_cases.description: SaaS founders use revenue financing to fund growth initiatives without diluting ownership.

use_cases.title: Acquisition and expansion loans
use_cases.description: Founders use term loans to fund larger capital needs like acquisitions or expansion over multi-year repayment terms.

use_cases.title: Bridging seasonal cash flow
use_cases.description: Seasonal CPG and brick-and-mortar businesses use merchant cash advances tied to a percentage of monthly revenue.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Founderpath?
paragraphs: Founderpath is a financing platform that provides non-dilutive capital to founders through revenue financing, term loans, and merchant cash advances. Instead of requiring equity, a pitch deck, or personal guarantees, Founderpath underwrites deals using business data and discloses rates upfront.
The company, founded in 2021, has funded over 740 companies and deployed more than $271M to date, according to figures published on its site.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Founderpath is for
paragraphs: Founderpath is aimed at software, brick-and-mortar, and CPG business owners with at least $500K in last-year revenue and recurring or repeat revenue streams who want capital without giving up equity or control.

name: Boopos
skip_reason: Product is discontinued for new customers: the site states Boopos is 'no longer accepting loan applications' and has joined Founderpath, with only existing loans still being serviced.

enrichment.short_description: Clearco provides non-dilutive funding structures for DTC ecommerce and SaaS businesses without equity, personal guarantees, or liens.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clearco offers non-dilutive funding for DTC ecommerce and SaaS brands, with fast approvals and no equity, personal guarantees, or liens.

features.title: Rolling and Fixed Funding Capacity
features.description: Businesses can draw non-dilutive capital through rolling or fixed funding structures based on their revenue.

features.title: Cash Advance and Invoice Funding
features.description: Additional funding structures cover cash advances and invoice-based financing needs.

features.title: Fast approvals
features.description: Clearco advertises approvals in as little as 24 hours using AI-supported underwriting.

features.title: Capped weekly payments
features.description: Repayment schedules use capped weekly payments with transparent, foreseeable terms.

features.title: Early payment option
features.description: Businesses can choose estimated 4, 5, or 6-month payment terms and pay early.

features.title: Platform integrations
features.description: Connects with Amazon, Stripe, BigCommerce, Square, Shopify, and PayPal to evaluate funding eligibility.

pros: No equity dilution, personal guarantees, or blanket liens required
Approvals advertised in as little as 24 hours
Multiple funding structures to match different cash-flow needs
Integrates directly with major ecommerce and payment platforms
Over $3B deployed to more than 10,000 funded brands, per company figures
cons: Requires 12+ months of consistent revenue and $100,000+ in monthly revenue to qualify
Limited to businesses incorporated in the U.S. with a U.S. business bank account
Specific rates and fees are not published and depend on underwriting
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: What types of businesses does Clearco fund?
faqs.answer: Clearco funds DTC ecommerce brands and SaaS businesses with at least 12 months of consistent revenue.

faqs.question: What is the minimum revenue requirement?
faqs.answer: Businesses generally need $100,000+ in monthly revenue and U.S. incorporation with an active U.S. business bank account.

faqs.question: Does Clearco require equity or collateral?
faqs.answer: No. Clearco funding is non-dilutive and does not require collateral or personal guarantees.

faqs.question: How fast can a business get funded?
faqs.answer: Clearco states approvals can happen in as little as 24 hours using AI-supported underwriting.

faqs.question: What platforms does Clearco integrate with?
faqs.answer: Clearco integrates with Amazon, Stripe, BigCommerce, Square, Shopify, and PayPal.

use_cases.title: Inventory and ad-spend funding for ecommerce brands
use_cases.description: DTC ecommerce brands draw rolling or fixed funding capacity to finance inventory and marketing spend.

use_cases.title: Non-dilutive growth capital for SaaS
use_cases.description: SaaS businesses use funding structures to grow without giving up equity.

use_cases.title: Smoothing cash flow with invoice funding
use_cases.description: Businesses with outstanding invoices use invoice funding to access capital sooner.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Clearco?
paragraphs: Clearco provides non-dilutive funding for direct-to-consumer ecommerce and SaaS businesses through structures including Rolling Funding Capacity, Fixed Funding Capacity, Cash Advance, and Invoice Funding. The company says funding decisions can happen in as little as 24 hours using AI-supported underwriting.
Clearco reports having deployed over $3B to more than 10,000 funded brands, with a stated 65% customer repeat rate.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Clearco is for
paragraphs: Clearco is built for U.S.-incorporated DTC ecommerce brands and SaaS businesses with at least 12 months of consistent revenue and $100,000+ in monthly revenue who want capital without diluting ownership.

enrichment.short_description: Numeral automates sales tax compliance, including nexus monitoring, registration, filing, and remittance, for ecommerce and SaaS companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Numeral automates sales tax nexus monitoring, registration, filing, and remittance for ecommerce and SaaS businesses in the US and 80+ countries.

features.title: Nexus monitoring
features.description: 24/7 monitoring that automatically flags and registers a business when it crosses new economic nexus thresholds.

features.title: Tax filing and remittance
features.description: Automated return preparation and on-time payment processing across US states.

features.title: Tax calculation API
features.description: Real-time tax rate calculation for checkout integration.

features.title: Exemption certificate management
features.description: Collection, validation, and verification of customer exemption certificates, with a free tier for up to 50 certificates.

features.title: Virtual mailboxes
features.description: Scans and helps respond to government tax correspondence on the business's behalf.

features.title: Compliance guarantee
features.description: Covers penalties and interest if Numeral misses a filing deadline.

pros: Free Monitoring plan covers nexus auditing and alerting with no cost
No long-term contracts; billing is month-to-month
Filing guarantee covers penalties and interest for missed deadlines
Integrates with major ecommerce, billing, and accounting platforms including Stripe, Shopify, Amazon, and NetSuite
Global coverage in 80+ countries available on the Pro plan
cons: Per-filing and per-registration fees can add up for sellers registered in many states
Advanced features like exemption certificate management and API access require the custom-priced Pro plan
Founding year is not disclosed on the site
Some states charge separate registration fees on top of Numeral's fees

faqs.question: Does Numeral have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. Numeral's Monitoring plan is free and includes nexus auditing, physical and economic nexus monitoring, and alerts when a business crosses into new states.

faqs.question: How much does filing cost with Numeral?
faqs.answer: The Standard plan charges $75 per filing and $150 per registration, billed month-to-month with no long-term contract.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan add?
faqs.answer: The custom-priced Pro plan adds exemption certificate management, API access, voluntary disclosure agreements, sales tax expert access, and coverage in 80+ countries.

faqs.question: What integrations does Numeral support?
faqs.answer: Numeral integrates with Stripe, Shopify, Amazon, Walmart, NetSuite, QuickBooks, eBay, WooCommerce, Orb, Chargebee, and other platforms.

faqs.question: What happens if Numeral misses a filing deadline?
faqs.answer: Numeral's compliance guarantee covers penalties and interest if a filing is missed.

use_cases.title: Multi-state nexus monitoring
use_cases.description: Ecommerce and SaaS companies use Numeral's free monitoring to track when they cross economic nexus thresholds in new states.
use_cases.audience: Finance teams at growing ecommerce and SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Automated filing and remittance
use_cases.description: Businesses use the Standard plan to automate filing and remittance across all US states they're registered in.
use_cases.audience: Companies with multi-state sales tax obligations

use_cases.title: Global tax compliance
use_cases.description: Companies expanding internationally use the Pro plan for coverage across 80+ countries and exemption certificate management.

features: Alerts when crossing into new states
Filing and remittance across all states

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Numeral?
paragraphs: Numeral is sales tax compliance software that automates nexus monitoring, registration, calculation, filing, and remittance for ecommerce and SaaS businesses across US jurisdictions and 80+ countries for VAT. It was founded by Sam Ross and Matt DuVall, a former Stripe and Notion engineer.
The platform is free for nexus monitoring and charges per filing and per registration on its Standard plan, with a custom-priced Pro plan for exemption certificates, API access, and global coverage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Numeral is for
paragraphs: Numeral is built for ecommerce brands and SaaS companies that need to track multi-state nexus and automate sales tax filing without a long-term contract.

enrichment.short_description: Anrok is a global sales tax and VAT compliance platform covering nexus monitoring, registration, calculation, filing, and remittance.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Anrok automates global sales tax and VAT compliance for SaaS and ecommerce, from nexus monitoring to filing, across 100+ countries.

features.title: Nexus monitoring and registration
features.description: Real-time monitoring of economic and physical nexus with automated registration across 100+ countries and 13,000+ US jurisdictions.

features.title: Real-time tax calculation
features.description: AI-powered tax calculation applied at checkout.

features.title: Automated filing and remittance
features.description: Handles filing and remittance across jurisdictions where a business is registered.

features.title: Reconciliation
features.description: Reconciles tax data across a company's financial systems.

features.title: Anrok Atlas AI assistant
features.description: An AI assistant that answers sales tax questions reasoned over a company's own account data and Anrok's tax knowledge base.

features.title: 40+ native integrations
features.description: Connects with billing, ERP, ecommerce, and HRIS platforms including Stripe, NetSuite, Salesforce, Zuora, Chargebee, QuickBooks, Workday, Shopify, and WooCommerce.

pros: Starter plans available for both SaaS ($100/market/month) and ecommerce ($50/market/month)
Anrok Atlas AI assistant answers tax questions grounded in a company's own account data
40+ native integrations across billing, ERP, ecommerce, and HRIS systems
Claims less than 1% of returns require revisions versus a stated industry average of 3%
Used by companies like Notion, Jasper, Vanta, and Anthropic, per case studies on the site
cons: Per-market pricing means costs scale as a business expands into new tax jurisdictions
Advanced features (multi-entity management, priority support, SSO) require the custom-priced plan
Founding year, headquarters, and team size are not disclosed on the site
High-volume Starter plan sellers may incur additional fees

faqs.question: How much does Anrok cost?
faqs.answer: Anrok's Starter plan is $100/market/month for SaaS companies and $50/market/month for ecommerce, with a per-market fee added for each new market activated. A Custom plan with volume-based pricing is also available.

faqs.question: What is Anrok Atlas?
faqs.answer: Anrok Atlas is an AI assistant that answers sales tax questions using a company's own account data combined with Anrok's tax knowledge base.

faqs.question: What jurisdictions does Anrok cover?
faqs.answer: Anrok covers 100+ countries and more than 13,000 US tax jurisdictions.

faqs.question: What platforms does Anrok integrate with?
faqs.answer: Anrok offers 40+ native integrations, including Stripe, NetSuite, Salesforce, Zuora, Chargebee, QuickBooks, Workday, Shopify, and WooCommerce.

faqs.question: Does Anrok support ecommerce, not just SaaS?
faqs.answer: Yes, Anrok expanded to support physical goods and ecommerce sellers in addition to SaaS billing models.

use_cases.title: Sales tax compliance for SaaS billing
use_cases.description: SaaS companies use Anrok to monitor nexus and automate tax calculation, filing, and remittance tied to subscription billing.

use_cases.title: Multi-market ecommerce tax compliance
use_cases.description: Ecommerce sellers use the ecommerce Starter plan to register and calculate tax as they expand into new markets.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted tax research
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Anrok Atlas to get answers to sales tax questions grounded in their own account data.

features: Same enhanced features as SaaS Custom

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Anrok?
paragraphs: Anrok is a sales tax and VAT compliance platform that manages the full lifecycle of tax compliance, from nexus monitoring and registration to real-time calculation, filing, remittance, and reconciliation, across more than 100 countries and 13,000 US jurisdictions.
The platform includes Anrok Atlas, an AI assistant that answers tax questions using a company's own account data, and offers separate pricing plans for SaaS and ecommerce businesses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Anrok is for
paragraphs: Anrok is aimed at fast-growing SaaS, ecommerce, and AI companies that need to manage sales tax and VAT compliance across many jurisdictions and billing systems.

enrichment.short_description: Zamp is a managed sales tax compliance service combining software with a dedicated tax expert team for registrations, filings, and notices.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zamp combines software and a dedicated tax team to manage sales tax registrations, filings, calculations, and notices for growing businesses.

features.title: Rooftop-level tax calculations
features.description: Calculates sales tax accuracy down to the exact customer address.

features.title: Nexus assessment
features.description: Reviews where a business has sales tax obligations, included in the free plan.

features.title: Notice management
features.description: Handles resolution of state tax notices on the business's behalf.

features.title: State registration handling
features.description: Manages state sales tax registrations for the business.

features.title: Dedicated tax specialists
features.description: Includes a team of tax specialists, including former auditors, for expert support.

features.title: Canada tax coverage
features.description: The U.S. + Canada plan adds GST/HST/PST/QST calculations and Canadian filing and reporting.

pros: Free plan offers a nexus assessment, product taxability review, and a 30-minute expert consultation at no cost
Two service models (fully managed "done for you" or reviewed "done with you") let businesses choose their level of involvement
Dedicated tax specialists, including former auditors, support notice resolution
Integrates with major platforms including Shopify, NetSuite, QuickBooks Online, Stripe, and Xero
Reports $300M+ in sales tax remitted and 100K+ on-time filings
cons: Specific dollar pricing for the paid U.S. and U.S. + Canada plans is not published; requires contacting sales
Founding year and company size are not disclosed on the site
SOC 2 compliance is listed as in progress rather than completed

faqs.question: Does Zamp have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zamp's free plan includes a nexus assessment, product taxability review, a 30-minute expert consultation, and API sandbox access.

faqs.question: How much do Zamp's paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Zamp does not publish fixed prices for its U.S. and U.S. + Canada plans; pricing is scoped based on a business's footprint and compliance needs via a sales conversation.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 'done for you' and 'done with you'?
faqs.answer: "Done for you" is a fully managed service, while "done with you" prepares filings for client review before submission.

faqs.question: Does Zamp support Canadian sales tax?
faqs.answer: Yes, the U.S. + Canada plan adds GST/HST/PST/QST calculations and Canadian filing, reporting, and specialist support.

faqs.question: What platforms does Zamp integrate with?
faqs.answer: Zamp integrates with Adobe Commerce, BigCommerce, Microsoft Dynamics 365, NetSuite, QuickBooks Online, Shopify, Stripe, WooCommerce, Xero, and a proprietary API.

use_cases.title: Assessing sales tax exposure
use_cases.description: Businesses use the free plan to get a nexus assessment and product taxability review before committing to a paid plan.

use_cases.title: Fully managed multi-state compliance
use_cases.description: Growing digital businesses use the 'done for you' service so Zamp's team fully manages registrations and filings.

use_cases.title: Notice resolution
use_cases.description: Businesses facing state tax notices use Zamp's specialist team to manage and resolve them.
use_cases.audience: Companies with existing sales tax notices

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zamp?
paragraphs: Zamp is a sales tax compliance service based in Miami, Florida, that pairs software with a dedicated team of tax specialists to handle registrations, filings, notices, and tax calculations for growing businesses.
The company offers a free plan with a nexus assessment and expert consultation, plus custom-priced U.S. and U.S. + Canada plans, and reports serving 1,200+ finance and accounting teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zamp is for
paragraphs: Zamp targets growing small-to-mid-size digital businesses in ecommerce, SaaS, and digital services that want a managed approach to sales tax compliance rather than handling filings entirely in-house.

enrichment.short_description: TaxJar automates sales tax calculation, reporting, and filing across sales channels, and is now owned by Stripe.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TaxJar automates sales tax calculations, reporting, and multi-state filing for ecommerce and SaaS sellers, backed by a 30-day free trial.

features.title: Real-time sales tax calculations
features.description: Calculates sales tax in real time across sales channels.

features.title: AutoFile
features.description: Automated filing and remittance of sales tax returns, with a set number of free AutoFile credits per plan per year.

features.title: Nexus insights
features.description: Tracks economic nexus and alerts sellers as they approach new state thresholds.

features.title: AI-driven product categorization
features.description: Uses AI to automatically categorize products for accurate tax treatment.

features.title: State registration assistance
features.description: Helps sellers register for sales tax permits in new states.

features.title: Flex fees
features.description: Allows temporary upgrades during high-sales months without a permanent plan change.

pros: 30-day free trial with no credit card required
Acquired by Stripe in 2021, backing it with additional resources and integration
Serves over 20,000 businesses across 11,000+ jurisdictions
4.7/5 rating on G2 with multiple G2 awards including Highest User Adoption and Fastest Implementation
Reports 100% on-time filing rate in 2025 and 99.999% historical uptime
cons: Starter plan is capped at 200 orders/month and only 3 import integrations
Additional AutoFile credits cost $50-$55 each beyond the plan's free allotment
Headquarters location is not disclosed on the site
Phone support is limited to the higher-priced Professional plan

faqs.question: Does TaxJar offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, TaxJar offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does TaxJar cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $39/month and the Professional plan is $99/month, both covering up to 200 orders/month, with a 10%+ discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Who owns TaxJar?
faqs.answer: TaxJar was acquired by Stripe in 2021 but continues to operate under the TaxJar name.

faqs.question: What does AutoFile include?
faqs.answer: AutoFile automates return preparation and filing; the Starter plan includes 2 free AutoFile credits per year and Professional includes 4, with additional credits available for a fee.

faqs.question: What integrations does TaxJar support?
faqs.answer: TaxJar integrates with Amazon, Shopify, WooCommerce, and offers a flexible API for custom integrations.

use_cases.title: Multichannel sales tax calculation
use_cases.description: Multichannel sellers use TaxJar to calculate accurate sales tax across each of their sales channels.

use_cases.title: Automated multi-state filing
use_cases.description: Growing businesses use AutoFile to automate return preparation and filing across the states where they're registered.

use_cases.title: Nexus threshold tracking
use_cases.description: Sellers use nexus insights to get alerted before crossing economic nexus thresholds in new states.

features: Dedicated onboarding and Customer Success Manager

long_form_blocks.heading: What is TaxJar?
paragraphs: TaxJar is a sales tax compliance platform, founded in 2013, that automates calculations, reporting, and multi-state filing for ecommerce and SaaS sellers. Stripe acquired TaxJar in 2021, and the product continues to operate under the TaxJar name.
TaxJar offers a 30-day free trial, followed by Starter ($39/month) and Professional ($99/month) plans, and reports serving more than 20,000 businesses across 11,000+ tax jurisdictions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who TaxJar is for
paragraphs: TaxJar is built for small and mid-market multichannel sellers across retail, food and beverage, medical, SaaS, and services industries that need automated sales tax calculation and filing.

enrichment.short_description: Avalara automates tax calculation, filing, classification, and compliance management across 190+ countries and multiple tax types.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Avalara automates tax calculation, filing, and compliance across sales/use, VAT, communications, and property tax in 190+ countries.

features.title: Multi-tax-type calculation
features.description: Calculates sales/use, property, VAT, communications, and occupancy taxes.

features.title: Automated returns and filing
features.description: Prepares and files tax returns automatically across jurisdictions.

features.title: E-invoicing and live reporting
features.description: Supports compliant e-invoicing and live tax reporting requirements.

features.title: Exemption certificate management
features.description: Manages collection and validation of customer exemption certificates.

features.title: Business license registration
features.description: Helps businesses register for required business licenses.

features.title: AI-powered classification
features.description: Uses AI agents, described as trained on billions of real-world transactions via Avalara's ALFA AI foundry, for item and tariff code classification.

pros: Covers tax compliance across 190+ countries and multiple tax types beyond just sales tax
Over 1,400 partner integrations including Shopify, NetSuite, SAP, Salesforce, and Oracle
AI-powered classification for item and tariff codes via its ALFA AI foundry
Serves segments from small business to enterprise across many industries
Reports 54B+ transactions processed annually with a stated 99.999% accuracy rate
cons: Pricing is not published; requires contacting sales for a quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the pages reviewed
Enterprise-oriented feature set and integration breadth may be more than smaller businesses need

faqs.question: How much does Avalara cost?
faqs.answer: Avalara does not publish fixed pricing on its site; businesses need to contact sales for a quote based on their needs.

faqs.question: What tax types does Avalara support?
faqs.answer: Avalara supports sales/use, property, VAT, communications, and occupancy tax calculation and compliance.

faqs.question: Does Avalara use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Avalara offers AI agents built on its ALFA AI foundry, which the company says incorporates 40+ models for tax calculation and item/tariff classification.

faqs.question: What systems does Avalara integrate with?
faqs.answer: Avalara has more than 1,400 partner integrations, including Shopify, Microsoft Dynamics, NetSuite, SAP, QuickBooks, Sage, Adobe Commerce, Salesforce, and Oracle.

faqs.question: How many countries does Avalara cover?
faqs.answer: Avalara says it can calculate, file, classify, and manage compliance across 190+ countries.

use_cases.title: Enterprise multi-tax compliance
use_cases.description: Enterprises use Avalara to manage sales/use, VAT, and other tax types across many jurisdictions from one platform.

use_cases.title: ERP-embedded tax calculation
use_cases.description: Businesses embed Avalara's tax calculation into ERPs and billing systems like NetSuite, SAP, and Salesforce.

use_cases.title: Automated item classification
use_cases.description: Retailers and manufacturers use AI-powered classification to assign accurate tax and tariff codes to large product catalogs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Avalara?
paragraphs: Avalara is a tax compliance platform that automates calculation, filing, classification, and compliance management across sales/use, property, VAT, communications, and occupancy taxes in more than 190 countries.
The platform integrates with over 1,400 partner systems, including major ERPs and ecommerce platforms, and uses AI agents built on its ALFA AI foundry for classification and calculation tasks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Avalara is for
paragraphs: Avalara serves businesses of varying sizes, from small business to enterprise, across industries including retail, manufacturing, software, construction, hospitality, and accounting firms that need multi-jurisdiction tax compliance.

enrichment.short_description: Quaderno automates sales tax, VAT, and GST compliance and invoicing for online businesses across 12,000+ tax jurisdictions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Quaderno automates sales tax, VAT, and GST compliance, real-time rate calculation, and localized invoicing for online businesses.

features.title: Tax registration tracking
features.description: Automatically tracks registration status and monitors nexus across jurisdictions.

features.title: Real-time tax calculation
features.description: Calculates tax rates in real time across 12,000+ jurisdictions.

features.title: Automated filing and reporting
features.description: Generates tax filing reports for over 12,000 jurisdictions.

features.title: Localized invoicing
features.description: Auto-generates localized, tax-compliant invoices.

features.title: Tax-compliant checkout
features.description: Integrates tax calculation directly into checkout flows.

features.title: API access
features.description: Provides an API for developers to build custom tax and invoicing workflows.

pros: 7-day free trial with no credit card required
Plans automatically upgrade to the next tier instead of charging surprise overage fees
Lower-tier plans (Startup and above) include unlimited users, jurisdictions, and integrations
Integrates with WooCommerce, Shopify, Stripe, GoCardless, PayPal, and Kajabi
Supports both English and Spanish
cons: Entry-level Hobby plan is capped at just 25 transactions/month and 1 jurisdiction
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site
Businesses over 2,500 transactions/month must contact sales for Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: Does Quaderno offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Quaderno offers a 7-day free trial that doesn't require a credit card.

faqs.question: How much does Quaderno cost?
faqs.answer: Plans range from $29/month (Hobby, 25 transactions) to $149/month (Growth, 2,500 transactions), with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed my plan's transaction limit?
faqs.answer: Quaderno automatically upgrades the account to the next subscription tier rather than charging overage fees.

faqs.question: What counts as a transaction?
faqs.answer: A transaction is any sale, refund, or purchase recorded within the app.

faqs.question: What integrations does Quaderno support?
faqs.answer: Quaderno integrates with WooCommerce, Shopify, Stripe, GoCardless, PayPal, and Kajabi, plus spreadsheet uploads and API access.

use_cases.title: Tax compliance for digital creators
use_cases.description: Digital creators use Quaderno to handle VAT/GST compliance and generate localized invoices for global customers.
use_cases.audience: Digital creators and small online sellers

use_cases.title: Automated invoicing for SaaS
use_cases.description: SaaS companies use Quaderno to auto-generate compliant invoices tied to subscription billing.

use_cases.title: Multi-jurisdiction tax reporting for marketplaces
use_cases.description: Marketplaces and platforms use Quaderno's unlimited-jurisdiction plans to manage tax reporting across many regions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Quaderno?
paragraphs: Quaderno is worldwide tax compliance software for online businesses, automating sales tax, VAT, and GST compliance, real-time rate calculation, and invoicing across more than 12,000 tax jurisdictions.
The platform offers a 7-day free trial and tiered monthly plans from $29 to $149 based on transaction volume, with unlimited users, jurisdictions, and integrations on all but its entry-level plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Quaderno is for
paragraphs: Quaderno is aimed at digital creators, ecommerce businesses, SaaS companies, platforms, marketplaces, and accountants who need tax-compliant invoicing and real-time tax calculation.

enrichment.short_description: Fonoa is an AI-driven tax technology platform for global indirect tax, covering VAT/GST calculation, e-invoicing, and returns.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fonoa provides AI-driven infrastructure for global indirect tax: ID validation, tax calculation, e-invoicing, returns, and audit tools.

features.title: Validate
features.description: Tax ID validation across 120+ countries.

features.title: Tax Engine
features.description: Automated tax calculation in 100+ jurisdictions with sub-200ms response times.

features.title: E-Invoicing
features.description: A unified API for compliant e-invoicing across 150+ countries.

features.title: Tax Returns
features.description: Automated multi-country tax return filing.

features.title: Knowledge
features.description: Real-time monitoring of tax legislation changes.

features.title: Audit
features.description: Tax data analysis tools to support audit readiness.

pros: API-first architecture covering the full indirect tax lifecycle: validation, calculation, e-invoicing, returns, and audit
Sub-200ms tax calculation response times, per the company
Broad jurisdiction coverage: 120+ countries for ID validation and 150+ for e-invoicing
Reports 100% customer retention and a 4.6/5 Gartner Peer Insights rating
Backed by recent growth, including a $110M raise and acquisition of PwC's Indirect Tax Edge
cons: Pricing is not published on the site; requires contacting sales
AI Agents product is not yet available
Founding year and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Fonoa cost?
faqs.answer: Fonoa does not publish pricing on its site; businesses need to contact the company directly for a quote.

faqs.question: What does Fonoa's Tax Engine do?
faqs.answer: Fonoa's Tax Engine automates tax calculation across 100+ jurisdictions with sub-200ms response times.

faqs.question: Does Fonoa support e-invoicing?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fonoa offers a unified API for compliant e-invoicing across 150+ countries.

faqs.question: Where is Fonoa headquartered?
faqs.answer: Fonoa is registered and headquartered in Dublin, Ireland.

faqs.question: Is Fonoa's AI Agents product available now?
faqs.answer: No, Fonoa's AI-driven compliance automation Agents product is listed as not yet available on its site.

use_cases.title: Global e-invoicing compliance
use_cases.description: Global businesses use Fonoa's E-Invoicing API to stay compliant across 150+ countries without building custom infrastructure per market.

use_cases.title: Real-time indirect tax calculation
use_cases.description: Companies embed Fonoa's Tax Engine into checkout or billing flows for real-time VAT/GST/sales tax calculation.

use_cases.title: Multi-country tax return filing
use_cases.description: Businesses use Fonoa's Tax Returns product to automate filing across multiple countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fonoa?
paragraphs: Fonoa is a Dublin, Ireland-based tax technology platform providing API-first infrastructure for global indirect tax, including tax ID validation, real-time tax calculation, e-invoicing, automated tax returns, legislation monitoring, and audit-readiness tools.
The company has raised $110M and acquired Indirect Tax Edge from PwC, and reports serving customers including Fin, Coda, Wellhub, and Remote.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fonoa is for
paragraphs: Fonoa is built for global businesses, including SaaS, marketplace, and platform companies, that need to automate indirect tax compliance such as VAT, GST, and e-invoicing across many jurisdictions.
